title: Are Women Really Better at Multitasking Than Men?
author: Jake Currie
text: Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisementPsychologyAre Women Really Better at Multitasking Than Men?There’s at least one glaring exceptionJCBy Jake Currie1:30 PM CDT on July 13, 2026Share on FacebookShare on X (formerly Twitter)Share on RedditShare on EmailShare on Bluesky1CommentsIn the past few decades or so, multitasking has morphed from a novel psychological concept for workaholics into a demanding way of life for all of us. With our attention constantly divided among screens, social media platforms, and notifications, it’s even invaded our leisure time—and doubly so if you’re a parent who works from home. Subscribe to skip adsFeatured VideoPowered By 10 Astronomers Discover Ceres’s Crust is Actually 90% WaterShareNextStayThat may be one reason that women, who still do most of the childcare, have long been credited with superior multitasking skills. But according to a new study in Psychological Research, men are just as good at multitasking (sort of).To test their respective multitasking capabilities, London psychologists recruited 41 men and 37 women to perform a series of simultaneous tasks while their performance was monitored. The multitaskers were asked to complete a simulated cooking task in a kitchen setting (mixing ingredients in a bowl). When the kitchen timer beeped, they had to grab a list with phone numbers, find one, and write it down before completing an additional number and letter matching assignment. Already have an account?Log inJoin to continue readingYou've run out of free articles.
